Information about DOIs and Crossref pref: Information about DOIs and Crossref prefixes
Prefix: Member Information Description: Statistics per prefix: total DOIs, document types, metadata coverage, and charts.
Prefix: DOI List Description: Advanced search and complete list of DOIs for a specific prefix.
DOI Search Description: Retrieve DOI metadata: authors, publisher, type, and deposit dates.
Member Search Description: Search institutions to retrieve their prefix, location, and data volume.
Prefix: DOIs and ORCIDs Description: Ranking of the most cited DOIs and ORCIDs of the institution.
Title: Title
Referenced Info: Number of times this DOI is referenced by other Crossref DOIs
Coverage Info: Percentage of content items that include this values in their metadata
Chart published date Info: Show the number of items per publication year
Chart first deposited date info: Show the number of items by year of deposit in Crossref
slow query message: This query takes some time because it makes multiple requests to the Crossref API to retrieve all the data. Please be patient.
results 10000: More than 10,000 results have been retrieved, please refine your search to limit the number of results and enable pagination.
Members list Info: Search institutions by name to access their profiles, DOI prefixes and scientific production volume.
AI Lab Description: Explore the core of current research and the evolution of the global scientific frontier through generative algorithms for monthly synthesis, quarterly horizon scanning, and innovation radar.
Advanced semantic analysis and Innovatio: Advanced semantic analysis and Innovation Radar with AI
Trends and deep analysis with AI: Trends and deep analysis with AI
Radar View Subtitle: Domain visualization. Outer bubbles (domains) group inner ones (specific trends).
Radar Description: This map visualizes the structure of global scientific knowledge:
Domains Explanation: Domains (Large bubbles): Represent key scientific areas grouping related topics.
Trends Explanation: Trends (Small bubbles): Specific topics or clusters that compose each domain.
Sampling Info: Analysis based on a sampling of the {count} most cited works from the last semester to identify high-impact topics.
Diameter Explanation: The diameter of inner bubbles indicates the volume of publications detected for each trend.
Detailed List Explanation: Below the chart you will find a detailed list of all trends ordered by number of works.
Daily Analysis Subtitle: Monthly high-impact synthesis and quarterly scanning of the global scientific focus.
Monitor Explanation: Unlike static reports, this daily analysis acts as a real-time monitor:
Serendipity Explanation: Maximizing serendipity, identifying unique creative intersections in the last 24h production.
Snapshots Explanation: These are snapshots that allow detecting weak signals and emerging micro-trends.
Daily Sampling Disclaimer: Based on a random sample of 100 Crossref documents. Due to limited sampling, some relevant topics might not be captured.
Analysis not available description: The analysis for this category has not been generated or there is not enough data for the selected date.
No sampling data: No sampling data found for this date.
Explore the most influential scientific: Explore the most influential scientific publications in the Crossref network.
This data is retrieved in real-time from: We work with the 30 most referenced publications. This data is retrieved in real-time from the Crossref REST API using the 'is-referenced-by-count' metric.
Synthesis Description: Identifies the most prominent and active research cores each month.
Horizon Description: Maps how the scientific frontier evolves by comparing consecutive periods.
Synthesis Rationale: We analyze the 300 articles with the highest citation impact from the last month to detect the most influential and consolidated research cores.
Horizon Rationale: We compare the 250 most cited articles of the last quarter with the 250 from the previous quarter (500 DOIs analyzed) to identify where the global scientific frontier is shifting.
Synthesis Bottom Footer: Each month, AI analyzes high-impact scientific articles to provide you with a clear and structured summary of the most relevant research topics.
Horizon Bottom Footer: Horizon Scanning is a methodology for examining signals of change to detect the birth of trends and the qualitative shift in the global scientific agenda.
